title: Rust: duty_cycle permille conversion may lose precision for fractional percentages
description:
In rust/lichen-core/src/duty_cycle.rs, duty cycle was changed from f32 fraction to u16 permille:

    // Before: duty_cycle: f32 = 0.01 (1%)
    // After: duty_permille: u16 = 10 (1%)

While avoiding float on embedded is good, permille cannot represent duty cycles finer than 0.1% (1 permille). Some regulations allow 0.01% duty cycle in certain bands, which would round to 0 permille.

Location: rust/lichen-core/src/duty_cycle.rs:29, 53
Risk: Low - 0.1% granularity is sufficient for EU868 regulations.
Fix: Document the precision limitation; consider using micropercentage (parts per 100,000) if finer resolution needed.
close_reason: Documentation only - 0.1% permille granularity is sufficient for EU868 regulations
